title: a crash when executing sql
description: Dear MonetDB Developers, I came across a crash in creating table in MonetDB. Whenever convenient, it would mean a lot if you could take a look. I sincerely appreciate your efforts in continuously improving MonetDB’s robustness. poc ```sql CREATE TABLE v0 ( v1 INT , v2 VARCHAR ( 77 ) NOT NULL CHECK ( CAST ( STDDEV_SAMP ( v1 / -1 ) OVER ( ORDER BY 15933342.000000 DESC ) AS INTEGER ) ) ) ; INSERT INTO v0 VALUES ( 83 , 27 ) , ( 96 , 2147483647 ) , ( -2147483648 , 76773466.000000 ) , ( 127 , 48396681.000000 ) , ( 0 , 16496086.000000 ) ; ``` ### Mclient output mclient generate the following ouput: unexpected end of file and mserver is terminated fish: Job 1, 'AFL_IGNORE_PROBLEMS=1 /usr/loca…' terminated by signal SIGABRT ### merovingian log output : mserver5: /home/MonetDB/sql/backends/monet5/rel_bin.c:1504: stmt *exp_bin(backend *, sql_exp *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, stmt *, int, int, int): Assertion `0' failed. ### Relevant source code
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE v0 ( v1 INT, v2 VARCHAR(77) NOT NULL, CHECK (v1 >= -2147483648 AND v1 <= 2147483647) );
SELECT * FROM v0 WHERE v1 IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE another_table ( ref_id INT, score FLOAT CHECK (score BETWEEN 0 AND 100) );
key_bug_features: Crash when creating table with invalid CHECK constraint
